name: CRISPR Edit Verifier
description: Predicts the expected Cas9 cut site for a given protospacer in a reference sequence, designs flanking Sanger sequencing primers, and returns an ICE/TIDE-style interpretation guide for verifying editing efficiency from sequencing trace data.

inputs.name: protospacer
inputs.description: The 20 bp DNA protospacer sequence (without PAM) used during editing. Standard bases A, T, G, C only.

inputs.name: reference
inputs.description: The reference DNA sequence in which the edit was made. Accepts a resource name (e.g. 'pBR322'), a raw sequence string, a FASTA-formatted string, or a GenBank-formatted string. The framework resolves these automatically.

inputs.name: primer_offset
inputs.description: Distance in bp from the cut site to the start of each sequencing primer. Default is 150. Reduce if the cut site is near the ends of the reference.

inputs.name: primer_len
inputs.description: Length of the designed sequencing primers in bp. Default is 20.

outputs.description: A dictionary containing the protospacer strand, cut position, forward and reverse sequencing primer sequences and positions, expected amplicon sequence and length, cut offset within the amplicon, and a step-by-step ICE/TIDE interpretation guide.
